qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via command injection in git commit-diff endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets the commit-diff route in server/routes/git.js where req.query.commit is interpolated into `git show ${commit}`; the advisory targets the user git-config route in server/routes/user.js where gitName and gitEmail are interpolated into `git config --global` commands.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or equivalent root cause. The advisory is shell command injection in POST /api/user/git-config caused by gitName/gitEmail command substitution reaching child_process.exec() in server/routes/user.js. The only qualifying finding is a separate shell command injection in GET /api/git/commit-diff caused by the commit query parameter reaching child_process.exec() in server/routes/git.js.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7fv4-fmmc-86g2 describes server/routes/user.js lines 58-59 using child_process.exec() with `git config --global user.name` and `git config --global user.email` built from gitName and gitEmail.
Finding os_command_injection_git_commit_diff reports server/routes/git.js:542 using execAsync(`git show ${commit}`, { cwd: projectPath }) with the commit query parameter.
The finding and advisory share a repository and vulnerability class, but the affected endpoint, file, parameter, git command, and vulnerable source-to-sink path differ.
blockers_to_higher_tier: Runtime tiering is not applicable because there is no advisory-matched qualifying finding.
